content.problemStatement:
Two strings AAA and BBB are equivalent (denoted by A∼BA \sim BA∼B) if they have the same lengths and AAA can be transformed into BBB by performing the following operation zero or more times: choose a substring of AAA which contains '1' an even number of times and reverse this substring.

You are given a binary string SSS. Find the number of different equivalence classes of the substrings of this string. In other words, find the smallest possible size of a set C\mathcal{C}C of binary strings with the following property: for each non-empty string RRR which is a substring of SSS (including SSS itself), there is a string X∈CX \in \mathcal{C}X∈C such that R∼XR \sim XR∼X.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains a single string SSS with length NNN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the number of equivalence classes among the substrings of the given string.
